children.heading: Assessments
children.label: a Assessments
children.text: The order shall provide that assessments shall be paid by a manufacturer if the manufacturer has manufactured concrete masonry products during a period of at least 180 days prior to the date the assessment is to be remitted.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Assessments required under the order shall be remitted by the manufacturer to the Board in the manner prescribed by the order.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 2 Timing
children.text: The order shall provide that assessments required under the order shall be remitted to the Board not less frequently than quarterly.

children.heading: Records
children.label: 3 Records
children.text: As part of the remittance of assessments, manufacturers shall identify the total amount due in assessments on all sales receipts, invoices or other commercial documents of sale as a result of the sale of concrete masonry units in a manner as prescribed by the Board to ensure compliance with the order.

children.heading: Initial rate
children.label: 1 Initial rate
children.text: The assessment rate on concrete masonry products shall be $0.01 per concrete masonry unit sold.

children.heading: Authority to change rate
children.label: A Authority to change rate
children.text: The Board shall have the authority to change the assessment rate. A two-thirds majority of voting members of the Board shall be required to approve a change in the assessment rate.

children.heading: Limitation on increases
children.label: B Limitation on increases
children.text: An increase or decrease in the assessment rate with respect to concrete masonry products may not exceed $0.01 per concrete masonry unit sold.

children.heading: Maximum rate
children.label: C Maximum rate
children.text: The assessment rate shall not be in excess of $0.05 per concrete masonry unit.

children.heading: Limitation on frequency of changes
children.label: D Limitation on frequency of changes
children.text: The assessment rate may not be increased or decreased more than once annually.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Late-payment and interest charges may be levied on each person subject to the order who fails to remit an assessment in accordance with subsection (b).

children.heading: Rate
children.label: 2 Rate
children.text: The rate for late-payment and interest charges shall be specified by the Secretary.
obligations of the United States or any agency of the United States;
general obligations of any State or any political subdivision of a State;
interest-bearing accounts or certificates of deposit of financial institutions that are members of the Federal Reserve System; or
obligations fully guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States.

children.heading: Assessment funds for regional initiatives
children.label: f Assessment funds for regional initiatives

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The order shall provide that not less than 50 percent of the assessments (less administration expenses) paid by a manufacturer shall be used to support research, education, and promotion programs and projects in support of the geographic region of the manufacturer.

children.label: A
children.text: Region I shall comprise Connecticut, Delaware, the District of Columbia, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Vermont, and West Virginia.

children.label: B
children.text: Region II shall comprise Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, and Virginia.

children.label: C
children.text: Region III shall comprise Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Kentucky, Michigan, Minnesota, Nebraska, North Dakota, Ohio, South Dakota, and Wisconsin.

children.label: D
children.text: Region IV shall comprise Arizona, Arkansas, Kansas, Louisiana, Missouri, New Mexico, Oklahoma, and Texas.

children.label: E
children.text: Region V shall comprise Alaska, California, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, Washington, and Wyoming.

children.heading: Adjustment of geographic regions
children.label: 3 Adjustment of geographic regions
children.text: The order shall provide that the Secretary may, upon recommendation of the Board, modify the composition of the geographic regions described in paragraph (2).
